title: Fuji
description:
a completely custom built single speed road bike, not technically fancy, but actually quite fancy. 
the two most unique things were an ideale type record 80 saddle that is quite worn, and actually custom punched and laced underneath, just above the rails. the second thing are the custom built cherry wood fenders. they are made locally by paul sykes and says "sykes" on the rear fender. they have leather arrow tip shaped mud flaps, and are only wide enough to accommodate up to 700c x 25 tires. 
the frame was unpainted and unfinished with a clear sealant. 
the wheel set is a black deep v origin8 single speed set with machined side walls.
the freewheel is white industries eno, 17 tooth if i remember correctly. 
the handlebars are soma condorina bars that look like cafe racer motorcycle bars. 
the grips are worn leather PDW (portland design works.
mountain style tektro break levers. 
the tires are red-walled continental vintage/classic or whatever term they used. 
there are black power grips on the pedals. 
there was a black minihaha brand saddle bag on the back of the saddle. 
black seatpost.  
black cable housing. 
black pedals. 
silver crank, silver origin8 48 tooth cog, i believe. 
the serial number will be under the bottom bracket, but i misplaced any pictures i'd previously taken of it.

title: 2012 Specialized Vita Step-Through
description:
My bike is a Specialized Vita Step Through matte black with hot pink trim.  The handle grips are black rubber with pink end tips. It has a black bell on the right, with a bold white sticker on top that says "I heart my bike". On the right hand side, it has "2013" written on the bar in silver marker. On the left, there is a PDW Coffee Cup Holder. The bike has a brand new Zefal front fender that was purchased the day before it was stole, along with a black seat lock, to a Milano Gel Saddle in Black. It has Shimano silver Sport/Traininig Road pedals, black kick stand, with a Topeak rack in the back. There is also a black Green Guru top tube rubber protector. Lastly, there is a "Bicycle Habitat - Soho, 250 Lafayette Street
New York, NY 10012" black/rainbow trim sticker on the base of the bike.

Many thank you's in advance.

title: 2014 Schwinn Speedster
description:
A ton of scuffs on either side of the front fork. I recently put on a black metal waterbottle holder on the front fork. Much newer front tire. Rear tire is a continental road tire. White leather saddle with a nickel sized section of leather ripped off around one of the metal pegs that holds the leather on the black plastic. A tiny led blinker on the rear post hanging on what looks like a hairtie. The rubber grip on the right side of the handlebar is always a little bit off of the bar, it slides around a little. Chain probably pretty rusty, I haven't oiled it recently. Either a 2014 or a 2015, I can't remember. LOOKS LIKE A FIXIE BUT ITS AN 3 SPEED INTERNAL HUB! Coaster brakes.

Overall pretty beat up, but I really loved it.

title: 2008 Marin Bikes Mount Vision
description:
Brief description: Full suspension bike, 26” wheels, white with distinctive Purple brake handles, purple disc brake callipers, purple brake discs and golden wheel hubs. Marin “Bear” logo on front of bike and bar end caps.

Components: 
Forks: 		Fox Float 120 RL
Rear shock:	Fox RP3 - Kashima coated
Handlebars:	BrandX
Seat Post: 	BrandX dropper post
Brakes: 	Hope Tech E4 Callipers (in purple) with Tech 2 brake levers (in purple)
Disc rotors:	Hope 180mm front, 160mm rear (in purple)
Wheel hub:	Hope Evo 2 (in gold)
Wheel rims: 	26” Mavic XC717 Disc
Tires:
		- front: Shwalbe Hans Dampf
		- rear: Specialized (?) 
Gears:		Shimano XT groupset:
		- rear derailleur and cogs: Shimano XT
		- Cranks: Shimano XT (silver)
		- front shifter (right hand handlebar): Shimano XT
		- RaceFace narrow-wide 1x 32t front cog (on crank arm)
Accessories: Black Crud Guard (mountain bike mud guard) on main down tube attached with black rubber bands, small bits of green/yellow tape in places. Green ‘Hope’ sticker on lower part of swing arm by rear gears. Black neoprene/velcro chain protector on lower part of swing arm - right hand side only.
The bike had significant upgrades from the stock purchase, notably the braking system was added as an upgrade, as were the wheels, dropper seat post, handlebar and front cog.

title: 2013 Cube Acid
description:
The bike is a large-frame hardtail with a red, grey and black design. It has Rockshox forks with red lettering, the front tyre is Cube's own with again, red and grey design. The back wheel is a DT SWISS replacement in black with white lettering.

The pedals are metal MTB pedals made by 'Stolen Bike Co' (the irony!), handlebar is black by Easton Monkey Bar. There is a 'pop-loc' feature for the suspension system with the trigger on the handlebar. 

The saddle is black and a ladies fit with a quick-release mechanism on the seat post to raise or drop the saddle. 

Tyres are grey and chunky, there's a bike bell on the left side and a black and blue torch-like light on the right. There's a bottle holder on the frame along with a mini pump clip and pump, if it's still there. 
There are a few scratches and dings on the frame - a sign of a well-used and loved bike.

title: 2024 Trek Marlin 5 - Gen 2
description:
Stolen Trek Marlin 5 – very distinctive color and setup, not a stock build.

Frame color: Volt Blue → Miami Green fade (bright, high-visibility gradient that really stands out)
Rear pannier rack/bag attached
Hybrid clip-in pedals (clip-in + flat combo)
Handlebar-mounted phone holder
Center/top tube bag mounted just behind the stem
Front and rear lights installed
Bell + horn on handlebars
Side-view mirrors on BOTH sides (uncommon and highly noticeable)

This bike is extremely recognizable due to the bright blue-to-green fade and the number of accessories. It looks more like a commuter/touring setup than a typical mountain bike.

If you see anything matching this description, even partially, please reach out.
